name: Greece non-dom 100,000 euro lump-sum tax (Article 5A)

headlineRate.sourceName: PwC Worldwide Tax Summaries, Greece, other tax credits and incentives
headlineRate.excerpt: individuals will pay a lump-sum tax of EUR 100,000 per tax year, irrespective of the amount of income earned abroad

durationYears.sourceName: PwC Worldwide Tax Summaries, Greece, other tax credits and incentives
durationYears.excerpt: for a maximum of 15 fiscal years

priorNonResidency.value: Must not have been a Greek tax resident for seven of the eight years prior to transferring tax residence to Greece.
priorNonResidency.sourceName: PwC Worldwide Tax Summaries, Greece, other tax credits and incentives
priorNonResidency.excerpt: the taxpayers were not Greek tax residents for the previous seven out of the eight years prior to transferring their tax residence to Greece

applicationWindow.value: Apply for the transfer of tax residence and non-dom status by 31 March of each tax year.
applicationWindow.sourceName: PwC Worldwide Tax Summaries, Greece, other tax credits and incentives
applicationWindow.excerpt: The relevant application for the transfer of tax residence and for obtaining the non-dom status has to be submitted by individuals by 31 March of each tax year

knownCatch.value: Non-payment of the full lump-sum tax in any year abolishes the regime, and worldwide income is then taxed under the general rules.
knownCatch.sourceName: PwC Worldwide Tax Summaries, Greece, other tax credits and incentives
knownCatch.excerpt: In the event of non-payment of the full amount of the lump-sum tax, the special regime will be abolished, and these persons will be taxed for their worldwide income in accordance with the general provisions

investmentRequirement.value: Must invest at least 500,000 euros in Greek real estate, businesses, or securities, completed within three years of the application.
investmentRequirement.sourceName: PwC Worldwide Tax Summaries, Greece, other tax credits and incentives
investmentRequirement.excerpt: they invested at least EUR 500,000 in real estate, businesses, or transferable securities or shares in legal entities based in Greece

summary: Greece's Article 5A non-dom regime lets a qualifying individual who transfers tax residence to Greece pay a flat lump sum of 100,000 euros per year on all foreign-source income, regardless of amount, for up to 15 years, in return for a 500,000 euro Greek investment. The figures below are screened, sourced, and dated. They are not advice.
